codeReview.summary: Clean, well-structured implementation of POST /v1/responses for native Codex Responses API passthrough. The code correctly defines ResponsesRequest/ResponsesResponse types and an optional ResponsesBackend interface. The handler properly resolves routes, type-asserts backend support, and forwards natively without translation. Both streaming and non-streaming paths are covered. CodexBackend implements the interface with raw body passthrough and 401 retry logic. Nine tests cover all expected behaviors including edge cases.

issues.description: The Responses handler uses a manual method check (r.Method != http.MethodPost) and the route is registered with r.Post() in main.go. Since Chi's r.Post() already rejects non-POST methods with 405, the manual check in the handler is redundant (but harmless). The ChatCompletions handler has the same pattern for consistency.

When iterating routing entries with multiple backends, the code returns after the first entry that doesn't support ResponsesBackend (writes a 400 error). If there are multiple backends in the chain and the first doesn't support responses but a later one does, it would never be reached. However, this matches the behavior of ChatCompletions and is consistent with the current routing design where entries typically resolve to a single backend.

ResponsesStream creates a new http.Client{} with no timeout for streaming, which is correct. However, this differs from the non-streaming doResponses which uses b.client (with configured timeout). This is a deliberate design choice and matches how streaming is typically handled, but the inconsistency is worth noting.

In handleResponsesStream, when scanner.Err() occurs after streaming has started and headers are already written, only a log message is produced. The client may receive a truncated stream without explicit error indication. This is standard practice for SSE (the connection simply closes) but worth noting.

summary: PASS — The implementation fully covers the feature requirements: POST /v1/responses endpoint registered, ResponsesRequest/ResponsesResponse types defined, optional ResponsesBackend interface with type assertion, native passthrough for both streaming and non-streaming, and proper 400 error for unsupported backends. CodexBackend correctly implements the interface with raw body forwarding and 401 retry. Nine comprehensive tests all pass. Code quality is good, follows existing patterns, and passes go vet. The only issues are minor/non-blocking: redundant method check (consistent with existing pattern), and the standard SSE stream truncation behavior on errors.
